title: Noticastrum decumbens
taxonomy: Noticastrum decumbens belongs to the family Annonaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This classification is based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Annonaceae family. Noticastrum decumbens is a member of the genus Noticastrum, which comprises several species of flowering plants found in the tropical regions of the Americas. The species is further classified into the order Magnoliales and the phylum Magnoliophyta, indicating its position in the plant kingdom. Noticastrum decumbens classification also reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment.

intro: Noticastrum decumbens, also known as "cachipira" or "cachiporra," is a plant species from the Anacardiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat fever, pain, and inflammation, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic effects. Bioactive constituents of Noticastrum decumbens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Noticastrum decumbens is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared to relieve pain and reduce fever.
